text: The business model of the modern web was not designed for distraction — it evolved toward it. Early online advertising simply replicated print: a banner in a margin, easily ignored. Click-through rates collapsed within years. Platforms learned that engagement, measured in time-on-site and return visits, was the only reliable proxy for ad revenue. This created a selection pressure: features that held attention survived; features that did not were cut. The result was an ecosystem shaped less by human intention than by the cold logic of optimization — a digital environment that emerged to capture minds because capturing minds paid.
question: According to the passage, why did online platforms evolve toward attention-capturing features?

options.text: Engagement metrics like time-on-site were the most reliable proxies for ad revenue
Platform designers intentionally set out to addict users from the start
Early banner advertising was too profitable to abandon quickly
Regulators required platforms to maximize user engagement
